title: Federal Aviation Administration
text:
Our Awards Programs
In the dynamic world of aviation, significant strides have been made in pilot proficiency, aircraft maintenance training, and unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) education. Programs like the WINGS - Pilot Proficiency Program, the AMT Awards Program, and the FAAST Team's initiatives for UAS enthusiasts exemplify the industry's commitment to continuous learning, skill enhancement, and embracing technological advancements. These efforts underline the dedication of professionals in the field to maintaining high standards of safety, efficiency, and innovation. Please review some of programs below:
WINGS Achievements
Over 55,000 pilots have earned at least one phase in the WINGS - Pilot Proficiency Program.
A total of 240,000 phases have been earned collectively by these pilots.
Find a member on the WINGS Achievement Roster
AMT Awards
Encourages Aircraft Maintenance Technicians (AMTs) and their employers to engage in initial and recurrent training.
More than 9,000 AMT Awards were earned in 2023.
Awards are issued based on the training received.
Review the participation requirements for the program (AC65-25)
